key_insight: The paper reformulates a time-discrete Cahn–Hilliard step into an equivalent four-field system by representing the weak Laplacian of the phase field through auxiliary scalar and vector fields.
engineering_relevance: This provides a conforming mixed finite element route that preserves mass conservation and energy decay while improving the analytical and numerical handling of the Cahn–Hilliard update.
method: Introduce auxiliary fields p and u so that -Δc is represented as 2p + div u.
Derive a continuous four-field formulation equivalent to the standard convex-splitting mixed time step.
State a conforming finite element discretization and prove one-step spatial estimates plus a stable auxiliary block estimate.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports manufactured-solution convergence tests along with verification of mass conservation, energy decay, and projected consistency of the auxiliary block.
watch_item: The auxiliary block includes an explicit weak-Laplacian recovery defect, so practical accuracy may depend on how this term is controlled in implementation.
paper_title: A four-field auxiliary reformulation of a Cahn-Hilliard time step: Analysis and conforming finite element discretization
